lessons.title: What Dashboard Symbols Are
lessons.content: Dashboard symbols are warning or information lights that appear on a car's instrument panel. They help drivers understand the condition of the vehicle and whether something needs attention.

lessons.title: Why Dashboard Lights Matter
lessons.content: Dashboard lights can warn the driver about safety issues, maintenance needs, or system activity. Learning these symbols helps drivers respond correctly and avoid damage.

lessons.title: Colors of Dashboard Lights
lessons.content: Red lights usually mean stop or urgent danger. Yellow or orange lights usually mean caution or service needed soon. Green or blue lights usually show that a feature is currently active.

lessons.title: Common Symbol Examples
lessons.content: Common dashboard symbols include the check engine light, oil pressure light, battery warning light, brake warning light, and coolant temperature light.

lessons.title: Oil Pressure Warning Light
lessons.content: The oil pressure warning light usually means the engine does not have enough oil pressure. This is serious because low oil pressure can quickly damage the engine.

lessons.title: Brake Warning Light
lessons.content: The brake warning light may mean the parking brake is on, brake fluid is low, or there is a brake system problem. Brake problems should be taken seriously right away.

lessons.title: Coolant Temperature Warning Light
lessons.content: The coolant temperature warning light means the engine is overheating. If this happens, the driver should pull over safely and let the engine cool down.

lessons.title: Battery Warning Light
lessons.content: The battery warning light usually points to a charging system issue. The battery, alternator, or wiring may not be working properly.

lessons.title: Check Engine Light
lessons.content: The check engine light means the car has detected a problem in the engine or emissions system. A steady light usually means service is needed soon. A flashing light may mean a more serious problem.

lessons.title: Low Fuel Light
lessons.content: The low fuel light means the fuel level is getting low and the driver should refuel soon.

lessons.title: Tire Pressure Warning Light
lessons.content: The tire pressure warning light means one or more tires may have low pressure. Driving with low tire pressure can reduce safety and fuel efficiency.

lessons.title: Seat Belt Reminder Light
lessons.content: The seat belt reminder light tells the driver or passenger to fasten their seat belt.

questions.question: What do dashboard symbols help a driver understand?
questions.answer: They help a driver understand the condition of the vehicle.

questions.question: What color dashboard light usually means urgent danger?
What color dashboard light usually shows a feature is active?
Why is the oil pressure warning light serious?
questions.answer: Because low oil pressure can quickly damage the engine.

questions.question: What should you do if the coolant temperature light comes on?
questions.answer: Pull over safely and let the engine cool down.

questions.question: What can the brake warning light mean?
questions.answer: It can mean the parking brake is on, brake fluid is low, or there is a brake system problem.

questions.question: What does the check engine light usually mean?
questions.answer: It means the car detected a problem in the engine or emissions system.

questions.question: What does the low fuel light mean?
questions.answer: It means the fuel level is low and the car needs fuel soon.

questions.question: What does the tire pressure warning light tell the driver?
questions.answer: It tells the driver that one or more tires may have low pressure.
